attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who attacks from reduced groups rather than peloton. Shows ability to drop companions on climbs but struggles to sustain solo efforts to the finish.
team_tactics: Operates independently in breakaways rather than relying on team support. No evidence of coordinated team lead-outs or positioning assistance in observed races.
terrain_preference: Excels in chaotic, unpredictable stages (cobbles, breakaway scenarios). Strong enough to drop riders on climbs within small groups but not a pure climber or sprinter.
vulnerability: Physical sustainability in fastest racing conditions - cramping in high-speed stages. Mental hesitation when positioned to win, particularly when isolated. Age-related endurance limits at 36.
key_patterns: Gains success from breakaways in reduced groups rather than mass sprints (2 observations)
Shows climbing strength to drop breakaway companions but fails to capitalize alone (2 observations)
Physical failures (cramp) and mental hesitation prevent converting strong positions to wins (2 observations)
Performs in chaotic Grand Tour stages where favorites' battles create breakaway opportunities (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed physical cracking under highest speeds and hesitation costing stage wins, suggesting age-related decline from 2022 success
